title: Release Engineering & DevOps Practices
frameworks: Release Checklist: tests pass → changelog updated → version bumped → docs current → deploy
Rollback Playbook: detect → assess → rollback → verify → postmortem
PR Review: passes tests, no secret leaks, docs updated, no architectural violations
Twelve-Factor App (Wiggins/Heroku): codebase, dependencies, config, backing services, build/release/run, processes, port binding, concurrency, disposability, dev/prod parity, logs, admin processes
Lighthouse Gate: performance budget check on every PR
concepts: Every release needs a pre-flight checklist verified before deployment
Rollback capability must be tested before you need it
Feature flags allow deployment without release — decouple ship from activate
Sprint experiments have a hypothesis, measurement criteria, and time-box
Lighthouse gates enforce performance budgets in CI — fail the build if perf regresses
Config lives in the environment, never in code (Factor 3)
Dev/prod parity eliminates 'works on my machine' bugs (Factor 10)
context: Extracted from ordostudio — production release engineering practices including a comprehensive release checklist, rollback playbook with step-by-step restore procedures, PR review checklists enforcing architectural invariants, feature flag infrastructure for safe graduated rollouts, and Lighthouse performance gates integrated into CI.
